steps.description: Select the "Declarative Agent – Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" option in the "New Project" dropdown within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "No Action" option in the dropdown menu within the "Create Declarative Agent" popup in Visual Studio Code's Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Click the "Default Folder" option in the "Workspace Folder" selection dropdown at the top of the Visual Studio Code window.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
#!/bin/bash

TARGET_DIR="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_XfRJT"
FILE_URL="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ayachensiyuan/vscuse-resources/refs/heads/main/DA-EK/Document.docx"
FILE_NAME="Document.docx"

# Create target directory if it doesn't exist
mkdir -p "$TARGET_DIR"

# Download file with timeout
curl -L --max-time 120 "$FILE_URL" -o "$TARGET_DIR/$FILE_NAME"
```

steps.description:
@code download file: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ayachensiyuan/vscuse-resources/refs/heads/main/DA-EK/Document.docx
save to the path: /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/${{var:app_name}}
Trigger the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P, while viewing the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension panel.
Type the text '>add capability' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to initiate the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" command.
Press Enter to select “Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability” from the Visual Studio Code command palette after typing “>add capability”.
Click the "Embedded Knowledge" option in the "Add Capability" dropdown menu within the Microsoft Copilot extension interface in Visual Studio Code.

Click the “manifest.json” file entry in the “Select Teams manifest.json File” dialog within Visual Studio Code, selecting the file located at /home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_6PgeC/appPackage.
Click the "Browse..." button in the "Add embedded knowledge files" dialog within Visual Studio Code, located under the file selection input.
Click the "Document.docx" file in the file selection dialog under the "vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_KAhFT" directory to select it for opening.
Click the "Open" button in the bottom-right corner of the file selection dialog to confirm and open the selected file "Document.docx".
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Provision" option under the "LIFECYCLE" section in the left sidebar of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.

Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code – Insiders, shown while viewing the "Overview of the Declarative Agent template" README with the "Provision" step selected in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar.
Type 'notifications: Show Notifications' into the VS Code command palette input at the top of the window.
Press Enter to select the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
@assertion notification "provision stage executed successfully" exist.
Click on the "Message Copilot" input text box in the M365 Copilot web chat interface.
Enter text 'hi' into the main chat input box below the prompt "Hi, what can I help you with?" in the M365 Copilot web interface.
Press the Enter key in the M365 Copilot chat input box after typing "hi" to submit the message.
Type the text "what's GPA of Sarah Miller" into the message input box at the bottom of the M365 Copilot chat interface, within the "Simple Greeting Exchange" conversation.
Press the Enter key in the text input box at the bottom of the M365 Copilot chat interface to submit the question "what's GPA of Sarah Miller".
Click the "Message Copilot" input box at the bottom of the Microsoft 365 Copilot chat interface to focus for entering a new message.
@assertion there's GPA score in the reply message.
